title: Selective prediction: field-shape confidence head with per-bucket calibrated abstention
hypothesis: The frozen §6 scorer has implemented selective prediction since bootstrap — a prediction with conf < 0.3 is an abstention excluded from that cell's median, and only coverage ≥ 0.2 is required — but the confidence pathway is still the bootstrap placeholder trained toward 'error < half the map extent', so conf saturates near 1 and coverage has been 1.000 in every one of the 24 cells of every experiment ever logged: the abstention mechanism §6 explicitly designed for ('a model that honestly abstains on bad frames is more useful than one that confidently guesses wrong') has never once fired. Meanwhile exp 14 (kept, 1856.69 -> 1638.3 m) established exactly the error-population structure that selective prediction exploits: its mixture analysis put only p ≈ 0.45–0.6 of field mass near truth, and its peak-commit decode splits predictions into a committed-correct subpopulation (a few hundred m) and committed-wrong misses (2–4 km) — confirmed by the predicted rise of the mean/median ratio from ~1.2 to ~1.35–1.4 in every area × bucket cell. Each cell's median therefore sits just on the wrong side of a bimodal error distribution: if the model can RANK its own crops by whether the field committed correctly, abstaining on the worst-ranked ones moves the median into the correct-commit mode. The ranking signal is already computed at inference and thrown away: the SHAPE of the decode distribution — sharpened-field peak mass, field entropy, and the distance between the sharpened and unsharpened decodes (a direct diffuseness/multimodality readout, the same diagnostic exp 14's analysis was built on). The confidence mechanism is a design family untouched in 14 experiments; it is deployment-honest (a UAV that skips an unsure fix coasts on dead reckoning for the 5–10 s until the next frame per §2's adaptive schedule — strictly better than ingesting a 2-km-wrong fix); and it can be made safe against the coverage-FAIL gate by construction: train the head as a calibrated hit predictor (BCE on 'committed decode within τ = 0.05 uv ≈ 350 m ≈ 1.6 grid cells') on detached inputs, then bake a per-area logit offset into the export, calibrated per lighting bucket on fresh-rotation TRAIN-split crops so that ≥40% clear the fixed 0.3 threshold in EVERY bucket — double the 0.2 coverage floor.
method: ONE focused change: the deployed confidence pathway. model/model.py — replace the placeholder Linear(48->1) conf head with a small MLP over [48-d GAP descriptor, sharpened-field peak mass, normalized field entropy, distance between sharpened and unsharpened decodes], ALL inputs detached so no confidence gradient can touch trunk/heads; conf = sigmoid(z - conf_shift) with conf_shift a registered buffer (0 during training); loss_fn's conf target tightens from err<0.5 to err<GOOD_ERR_UV=0.05 (weight 0.1->0.3). model/train.py — after the epoch loop, per-area calibration on ~400 fresh-rotation TRAIN-split crops per lighting bucket sets conf_shift so the scorer's fixed 0.3 threshold keeps ≥40% in the worst bucket, then export. Trunk, layout head, dark expert, gate, β=3 decode, Gaussian-CE and coordinate L2: unchanged.
expected_outcome: If the field-shape statistics rank commit-correctness with even moderate skill (AUC ≥ ~0.7), each cell's median moves toward the committed-correct subpopulation: predicted primary worst-case median 900–1400 m (from 1638.3 m), with the largest relative gains in the night cells (most diffuse fields, so the most filterable misses — night is the current worst bucket in all four areas). Coverage (secondary, reported honestly) drops from 1.0 to ~0.4–0.75 per cell; calibration keep-rates per bucket are logged in train_info.json. Null: confidence uninformative -> the kept subset is a near-random sample, median unchanged ≈1638 m, revert. Bounded worst case: some eval cell's coverage < 0.2 -> 1e9 -> revert; made unlikely by the 40%-keep calibration (2× the floor). If supported, the loop's subsequent battlefield changes shape: further gains must raise field quality so coverage climbs at fixed accuracy — both numbers are in every cell's log.
eli5: Until now the drone's navigator shouted out a position guess for every single photo, even the hopeless ones, and those wild guesses dragged its accuracy score down. This change teaches it to look at its own heat map of candidate locations and notice the difference between one crisp hotspot (sure) and a washed-out smear of weak maybes (not sure) — and in the unsure case to say 'no fix this time', letting the drone coast on its last known position for a few extra seconds, which the mission profile explicitly allows. The scoring rules have always worked this way — only answers it commits to count, as long as it doesn't stay silent too often — so we tuned it to keep answering at least 40% of the time in every lighting condition, double the required minimum.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small (first 9 blocks, 190k params), gently fine-tuned

stages.name: Layout summary
stages.detail: a slim extra layer condenses the 8×8 feature grid into a 512-number code that remembers what sits where in the crop; the texture average (48 numbers) is kept alongside

stages.name: Lighting gate
stages.detail: a tiny network reads the crop's own average brightness together with the 48-number texture summary and outputs one number: how night-like is this shot?

stages.name: Probability map
stages.detail: two scorers read the crop — the layout-aware scorer and the brightness-only low-light scorer — and the gate blends their per-cell guesses into one heat map

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: the heat map's contrast is turned up first (peaks boosted, faint background suppressed), then the answer is the balance point of the sharpened map — committing to the strongest hotspot instead of averaging every faint guess across the whole city

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: the model now inspects the shape of its own heat map — one crisp hotspot means sure, a washed-out smear means unsure — and below a tuned bar it says 'no fix' instead of guessing; the bar is set so it still answers at least 40% of the time in every lighting condition

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: the confidence score is now graded on one simple question — did the committed answer land within ~350 m? — and after training, the 'sure enough to answer' bar is tuned on fresh practice crops from every lighting condition; the position-learning signals are untouched

implementation_brief:
Two files change: model/model.py and model/train.py. Nothing else. Do not touch pipeline/, autoresearch/, areas.yaml, or anything listed in /FROZEN; never read the 'eval' split or the hamburg area.

=== model/model.py ===
1. Module constants: next to DECODE_BETA add
   GOOD_ERR_UV = 0.05  # confidence 'hit' radius in normalized map units (~350 m on a ~7 km area, ~1.6 grid cells)
   Leave GRID_K, TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS, DECODE_BETA and the pretrained-trunk constants unchanged.
2. TinyLocNet.__init__: replace `self.conf_head = nn.Linear(feat_ch, 1)` with
   self.conf_head = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(feat_ch + 3, 32), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(32, 1))
   and add `self.register_buffer('conf_shift', torch.zeros(1))` (calibration offset: 0 during training, set post-training by train.py, baked into the ONNX export because it is a buffer). Everything else in __init__ unchanged.
3. TinyLocNet.forward: keep everything through the computation of logits, p = softmax(DECODE_BETA * logits), u, v EXACTLY as is. Then replace the single confidence line (`conf = torch.sigmoid(self.conf_head(f))`) with:
   p1 = torch.softmax(logits, dim=1)                                  # unsharpened field
   u1 = (p1 * self.cell_u).sum(dim=1, keepdim=True)
   v1 = (p1 * self.cell_v).sum(dim=1, keepdim=True)
   pd = p.detach()
   peak = pd.max(dim=1, keepdim=True).values                          # sharpened-field peak mass
   ent = -(pd * torch.log(pd + 1e-9)).sum(dim=1, keepdim=True) / float(np.log(self.grid_k * self.grid_k))   # normalized entropy in [0,1]
   gap = torch.sqrt((u.detach() - u1.detach()) ** 2 + (v.detach() - v1.detach()) ** 2 + 1e-12)              # committed-vs-mean decode disagreement
   z = self.conf_head(torch.cat([f.detach(), peak, ent, gap], dim=1))
   conf = torch.sigmoid(z - self.conf_shift)
   The output `torch.cat([u, v, conf], dim=1)` and the return_logits branch stay exactly as they are. IMPORTANT: every conf-head input is detached — the confidence objective must not backpropagate into the trunk, heads, or gate. (Intended, pre-registered side effect: the OLD conf-BCE gradient into f disappears; the localization path no longer receives any confidence gradient at all.) All new ops (ReduceMax, Log, Sqrt, Sigmoid, broadcast subtract of a [1] buffer) are ONNX-opset-17-safe; export_onnx stays unchanged.
4. loss_fn: change the conf target from `good = (coord_err.sqrt() < 0.5).float()` to `good = (coord_err.sqrt() < GOOD_ERR_UV).float()`, and the conf term weight from 0.1 to 0.3 (the head is detached, so this weight trains only the head). Replace the stale comment above `good` with one sentence: conf is now a calibrated hit predictor for selective prediction — the frozen scorer treats conf < 0.3 as abstention and requires coverage >= 0.2 per cell. CE term and coordinate L2 term: unchanged. Update the module docstring with a short 'Exp 15' paragraph in the same style as the existing history.

=== model/train.py ===
1. Add module constants near the imports:
   CAL_CROPS_PER_BUCKET = 400
   MIN_KEEP_RATE = 0.40          # per-bucket keep floor at calibration (2x the scorer's 0.2 coverage floor)
   SCORER_CONF_THRESHOLD = 0.3   # mirrors pipeline/score.py's frozen CONF_THRESHOLD; restated here, not imported
2. New function, placed after load_training_tensors:
   def calibrate_conf_shift(model, area, data_dir, device, rng) -> dict:
   - meta = load_meta(area, data_dir); crops = list_crops(area, meta['width'], meta['height'], 'train')   # TRAIN split only — never 'eval', never hamburg
   - model.eval(); inside torch.no_grad(): for each bucket in LIGHTING_BUCKETS: open the relight PNG exactly as load_training_tensors does; pick min(CAL_CROPS_PER_BUCKET, len(crops)) indices via rng.choice(len(crops), size=..., replace=False); extract each crop with extract_crop(img, c['cx'], c['cy'], angle) at a FRESH angle = float(rng.uniform(0, 360)) (fresh headings approximate unseen-view conditions better than reusing training-tensor views); run the model in minibatches of 64 using the same uint8-to-float conversion as the training loop (.permute(0, 3, 1, 2).contiguous().float().div_(255.0)); collect the conf column (output index 2) into a per-bucket array.
   - conf_shift is still 0 here, so recover raw head logits: c = np.clip(conf_values, 1e-6, 1 - 1e-6); z = np.log(c / (1 - c)).
   - per bucket: t_bucket = float(np.quantile(z_bucket, 1.0 - MIN_KEEP_RATE)); then T = min(t_bucket over buckets).
   - inside torch.no_grad(): model.conf_shift.fill_(T - float(np.log(SCORER_CONF_THRESHOLD / (1.0 - SCORER_CONF_THRESHOLD))) - 1e-4)
     (After this, exported conf >= 0.3 exactly when raw z > T - 1e-4, so every bucket keeps >= 40% of its calibration crops; brighter buckets keep more.)
   - return {'conf_shift': float(model.conf_shift.item()), 'cal_logit_threshold': float(T), 'cal_keep_rate_per_bucket': {bucket: float(np.mean(z_bucket > T - 1e-4)) for each bucket}}
3. train_area: after the epoch loop and BEFORE the existing `export_onnx(model.cpu(), ...)` lines, call `cal = calibrate_conf_shift(model, area, data_dir, device, rng)` (model still on device), then proceed to the existing model.cpu() + export (the buffer moves with the model), and merge `cal` into the returned info dict (info.update(cal) style) so it lands in train_info.json. Update the file docstring with a short 'Exp 15' paragraph.
4. Everything else unchanged: load_training_tensors, optimizer param groups (trunk lr 1e-4 / head lr 1e-3 — the new conf-head params fall into the existing 'head' group automatically), epochs/batching/seed handling, CLI flags and defaults.

=== Contracts to preserve (the frozen scorer depends on them) ===
- CLI: python -m model.train --area <area> --out-dir <dir> [--data-dir D] [--epochs N] [--max-crops-per-bucket M] [--seed S]; appends one info dict to <out-dir>/train_info.json.
- Export: one ONNX per area at <out>/models/<area>.onnx; input 'frame' 1x3x128x128 float32 in [0,1]; output [[u, v, conf]] with u, v normalized map coords and conf in [0,1]; opset 17, dynamo=False.
- Deployment gates: exported ONNX <= 4 MiB per area (currently 3.29 MiB; this change adds ~2k params, well under 10 KB) and host latency proxy <= 250 ms (currently ~0.8 ms).
- estimate_position() in model/model.py: unchanged.
